description: A fellow web-developer has left you a small demo project under /home/user/webapp. You have been asked to perform a **very quick static security sweep** that only looks for the two most obviously dangerous PHP functions: eval( and exec(. Your goals: 1. Search every PHP file located anywhere inside the directory /home/user/webapp for the literal strings “eval(” or “exec(”. (The match must be case-sensitive; do not look for upper-case variants.) 2. Create the directory /home/user/web_security_scan (if it does not already exist) and write a file named /home/user/web_security_scan/quick_scan.log that summarises the findings in the **exact format** described below. 3. After you have written the file, output its full contents to the terminal so the developer can read it right away. Required log-file format (this **must match exactly**, including capitalisation, blank lines and the order of the lines, because an automated test will read the file): Line 1: SCAN SUMMARY Line 2: Total insecure occurrences: (← one completely blank line) From line 4 onward: one line per occurrence, sorted in plain ASCII/lexicographical order by the **relative** file name. Each line must look like <relative_path>:<line_number>:<matched_function> where • <relative_path> is the path starting directly under /home/user/webapp (no leading “./”, no leading slash), • <line_number> is the line number reported by your search tool, and • <matched_function> is either eval( or exec( exactly as it appears in the file. Example of the required shape (the numbers and paths below are only an illustration): SCAN SUMMARY Total insecure occurrences: 5 api/handler.php:12:exec( api/handler.php:29:eval( index.php:42:eval( ... Constraints and hints: • You are expected to accomplish the task with **no more than 2–3 shell commands**. • Do not try to install any additional software; standard GNU/Linux utilities available in a minimal container are enough. • Remember to create the target directory before writing the log. • When you are done, use a command to display the contents of /home/user/web_security_scan/quick_scan.log so that the developer sees the report immediately in the console.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth for automated verification.
        These values MUST remain secret from the agent.
    -->

    <!-- Pre-existing project layout -->
    Directory: /home/user/webapp

    File: /home/user/webapp/index.php  (18 lines)
        Line 14 contains exactly:      eval($_GET['run']);

    File: /home/user/webapp/admin/tools.php  (70 lines)
        Line 45 contains exactly:      exec($cmd);
        Line 60 contains exactly:      eval($dangerous);

    These are the only occurrences of the literals “eval(” or “exec(”
    anywhere under /home/user/webapp.

    Expected artefact created by the agent:
    File: /home/user/web_security_scan/quick_scan.log
    Contents (including the single blank line after line 2):

        SCAN SUMMARY
        Total insecure occurrences: 3

        admin/tools.php:45:exec(
        admin/tools.php:60:eval(
        index.php:14:eval(

    The automated test will:
      • Verify that /home/user/web_security_scan/quick_scan.log exists.
      • Verify that its contents are byte-for-byte identical to the
        block shown above.
      • Verify that the agent printed the same contents to STDOUT.
